Social Proof
Most Common Mistakes
● If we are not careful, we can easily mis-use social
proof; by doing so it will actually work against us.
○ For example showing the majority is doing the
undesired behavior.
● Underestimating the importance of similarity
between the target of the persuasive appeal and the
other people you're referencing when trying to create
social proof.
● Having no knowledge of future social proof and
trends.
● Confusing authority (famous people/“influencers”) for
social proof.
● Not knowing how to frame the most powerful
sentence of social proof. Remember, there are 3 things
to consider:
1. Describe similar others
2. Name the desired behavior literally
3. Use exact numbers to boost trust
www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 1 --
